contentBlocks.original:
प्रजा पतीनां प्रथमं तेजसां पुरुषं प्रभुम् ।
भुवनं भूर्भुवं देवं सर्वलोकेश्वरं प्रभुम्॥ 1
contentBlocks.transliteration:
prajā patīnāṃ prathamaṃ tējasāṃ puruṣaṃ prabhum ।
bhuvanaṃ bhūrbhuvaṃ dēvaṃ sarvalōkēśvaraṃ prabhum॥ 1
contentBlocks.translation: I praise the first among lords of creation, the supreme among the radiant, the Purusha — the divine lord of the universe, ruler of the earth and the sky, lord of all worlds.

contentBlocks.original:
ईशानां वरदं पार्थ दृष्णवानसि शङ्करम् ।
तं गच्च शरणं देवं वरदं भवनेश्वरम् ॥ 2
contentBlocks.transliteration:
īśānāṃ varadaṃ pārtha dṛṣṇavānasi śaṅkaram ।
taṃ gachcha śaraṇaṃ dēvaṃ varadaṃ bhavanēśvaram ॥ 2
contentBlocks.translation: O Partha (Arjuna), you have seen Shankara — the lord of all lords, the granter of boons. Go to him for refuge — to that divine, boon-granting lord of all worlds.

contentBlocks.original:
महादेवं महात्मान मीशानं जटिलं शिवम् ।
त्य्रक्षं महाभुजं रुद्रं शिखिनं चीरवासनम् ॥ 3
contentBlocks.transliteration:
mahādēvaṃ mahātmāna mīśānaṃ jaṭilaṃ śivam ।
tyrakṣaṃ mahābhujaṃ rudraṃ śikhinaṃ chīravāsanam ॥ 3
contentBlocks.translation: The great god, the great soul, the all-ruling, the matted-locked, the auspicious Shiva; the three-eyed, the mighty-armed Rudra, wearing bark garments.

contentBlocks.original:
महादेवं हरं स्थाणुं वरदं भवनेश्वरम् ।
जगत्र्पाधानमधिकं जगत्प्रीतमधीश्वरम् ॥ 4
contentBlocks.transliteration:
mahādēvaṃ haraṃ sthāṇuṃ varadaṃ bhavanēśvaram ।
jagatrpādhānamadhikaṃ jagatprītamadhīśvaram ॥ 4
contentBlocks.translation: Mahadeva, Hara, the immovable pillar, the boon-granting lord of all worlds; the supreme foundation of the universe, the greatly beloved, the highest sovereign.

contentBlocks.original:
जगद्योनिं जगद्द्वीपं जयनं जगतो गतिम् ।
विश्वात्मानं विश्वसृजं विश्वमूर्तिं यशस्विनम् ॥ 5
contentBlocks.transliteration:
jagadyōniṃ jagaddvīpaṃ jayanaṃ jagatō gatim ।
viśvātmānaṃ viśvasṛjaṃ viśvamūrtiṃ yaśasvinam ॥ 5
contentBlocks.translation: Womb of the world, island of the world, victor of the world, path of the world; the universal self, creator of the universe, cosmic form, the glorious one.

contentBlocks.original:
विश्वेश्वरं विश्ववरं कर्माणामीश्वरं प्रभुम् ।
शम्भुं स्वयम्भुं भूतेशं भूतभव्यभवोद्भवम् ॥ 6
contentBlocks.transliteration:
viśvēśvaraṃ viśvavaraṃ karmāṇāmīśvaraṃ prabhum ।
śambhuṃ svayambhuṃ bhūtēśaṃ bhūtabhavyabhavōdbhavam ॥ 6
contentBlocks.translation: Lord of the universe, best in the universe, lord of all actions; Shambhu (giver of peace), self-born, lord of all beings, the origin of the past, present and future.

contentBlocks.original:
योगं योगेश्वरं शर्वं सर्वलोकेश्वरेश्वरम् ।
सर्वश्रेष्टं जगच्छ्रेष्टं वरिष्टं परमेष्ठिनम् ॥ 7
contentBlocks.transliteration:
yōgaṃ yōgēśvaraṃ śarvaṃ sarvalōkēśvarēśvaram ।
sarvaśrēṣṭaṃ jagachChrēṣṭaṃ variṣṭaṃ paramēṣṭhinam ॥ 7
contentBlocks.translation: Yoga itself, lord of yoga, Sharva (archer), lord of lords of all worlds; best among all, best among the universe, the most exalted, the supreme standing one.

contentBlocks.original:
लोकत्रय विधातारमेकं लोकत्रयाश्रयम् ।
सुदुर्जयं जगन्नाथं जन्ममृत्यु जरातिगम् ॥ 8
contentBlocks.transliteration:
lōkatraya vidhātāramēkaṃ lōkatrayāśrayam ।
sudurjayaṃ jagannāthaṃ janmamṛtyu jarātigam ॥ 8
contentBlocks.translation: Creator of the three worlds, the one, the refuge of the three worlds; the unconquerable, lord of all, beyond birth, death and old age.

contentBlocks.original:
ज्ञानात्मानां ज्ञानगम्यं ज्ञानश्रेष्ठं सुदर्विदम् ।
दातारं चैव भक्तानां प्रसादविहितान् वरान् ॥ 9
contentBlocks.transliteration:
jñānātmānāṃ jñānagamyaṃ jñānaśrēṣṭhaṃ sudarvidam ।
dātāraṃ chaiva bhaktānāṃ prasādavihitān varān ॥ 9
contentBlocks.translation: Accessible only to those of knowledge, attainable by knowledge, supreme among the knowledgeable; bestower of the boons sought by devotees, the graciously ordained gifts.

contentBlocks.original:
तस्य पारिषदा दिव्यारूपै र्नानाविधै र्विभोः ।
वामना जटिला मुण्डा ह्रस्वग्रीव महोदराः ॥ 10
contentBlocks.transliteration:
tasya pāriṣadā divyārūpai rnānāvidhai rvibhōḥ ।
vāmanā jaṭilā muṇḍā hrasvagrīva mahōdarāḥ ॥ 10
contentBlocks.translation: His attendants are of many divine forms — dwarfs, matted-locked, shaven, with short necks and large bellies.

contentBlocks.original:
महाकाया महोत्साहा महाकर्णास्तदा परे ।
आननैर्विकृतैः पादैः पार्थवेषैश्च वैकृतैः ॥ 11
contentBlocks.transliteration:
mahākāyā mahōtsāhā mahākarṇāstadā parē ।
ānanairvikṛtaiḥ pādaiḥ pārthavēṣaiścha vaikṛtaiḥ ॥ 11
contentBlocks.translation: Of huge bodies, great energy, enormous ears — some others with grotesque faces, feet, and varied disguises.

contentBlocks.original:
ईदृशैस्स महादेवः पूज्यमानो महेश्वरः ।
सशिवस्तात तेजस्वी प्रसादाद्याति तेऽग्रतः ॥ 12
contentBlocks.transliteration:
īdṛśaissa mahādēvaḥ pūjyamānō mahēśvaraḥ ।
saśivastāta tējasvī prasādādyāti tē'grataḥ ॥ 12
contentBlocks.translation: Thus honoured, the great Mahadeva, Maheshvara — together with Shiva, radiant, moves before you by his grace, O son.

contentBlocks.original:
तस्मिन् घोरे सदा पार्थ सङ्ग्रामे रोमहर्षिणे ।
द्रौणिकर्ण कृपैर्गुप्तां महेष्वासैः प्रहारिभिः ॥ 13
contentBlocks.transliteration:
tasmin ghōrē sadā pārtha saṅgrāmē rōmaharṣiṇē ।
drauṇikarṇa kṛpairguptāṃ mahēṣvāsaiḥ prahāribhiḥ ॥ 13
contentBlocks.translation: In that terrible battle, hair-raising, O Partha — guarded by Drona's son (Ashvatthama), Karna and Kripa, those great archers who strike down all.

contentBlocks.original:
कस्तां सेनां तदा पार्ध मनसापि प्रधर्षयेत् ।
ऋते देवान्महेष्वासाद्बहुरूपान्महेश्वरात् ॥ 14
contentBlocks.transliteration:
kastāṃ sēnāṃ tadā pārdha manasāpi pradharṣayēt ।
ṛtē dēvānmahēṣvāsādbahurūpānmahēśvarāt ॥ 14
contentBlocks.translation: Who could challenge that army even in thought, O Partha, except the gods and that many-formed great lord Maheshvara?

contentBlocks.original:
प्थातुमुत्सहते कश्चिन्नतस्मिन्नग्रतः स्थिते ।
न हि भूतं समं तेन त्रिषु लोकेषु विद्यते ॥ 15
contentBlocks.transliteration:
pthātumutsahatē kaśchinnatasminnagrataḥ sthitē ।
na hi bhūtaṃ samaṃ tēna triṣu lōkēṣu vidyatē ॥ 15
contentBlocks.translation: No one is equal to him in the three worlds. One standing before him cannot endure even to think of attack.

contentBlocks.original:
गन्धे नापि हि सङ्ग्रामे तस्य कृद्दस्य शत्रवः ।
विसञ्ज्ञा हत भूयिष्टा वेपन्तिच पतन्ति च ॥ 16
contentBlocks.transliteration:
gandhē nāpi hi saṅgrāmē tasya kṛddasya śatravaḥ ।
visañjñā hata bhūyiṣṭā vēpanticha patanti cha ॥ 16
contentBlocks.translation: In battle, enemies of that wrathful one become senseless, mostly killed and fallen, trembling and collapsing.

contentBlocks.original:
तस्मै नमस्तु कुर्वन्तो देवा स्तिष्ठन्ति वैदिवि ।
ये चान्ये मानवा लोके येच स्वर्गजितो नराः ॥ 17
contentBlocks.transliteration:
tasmai namastu kurvantō dēvā stiṣṭhanti vaidivi ।
yē chānyē mānavā lōkē yēcha svargajitō narāḥ ॥ 17
contentBlocks.translation: The gods stand before him, ever offering salutations; and other men of this world, and those who have won the heavens.

contentBlocks.original:
ये भक्ता वरदं देवं शिवं रुद्रमुमापतिम् ।
इह लोके सुखं प्राप्यते यान्ति परमां गतिम् ॥ 18
contentBlocks.transliteration:
yē bhaktā varadaṃ dēvaṃ śivaṃ rudramumāpatim ।
iha lōkē sukhaṃ prāpyatē yānti paramāṃ gatim ॥ 18
contentBlocks.translation: Those who are devoted to the boon-granting divine Shiva, Rudra, the husband of Uma — they attain happiness in this world and then the supreme destination.

contentBlocks.original:
नमस्कुरुष्व कौन्तेय तस्मै शान्ताय वै सदा ।
रुद्राय शितिकण्ठाय कनिष्ठाय सुवर्चसे ॥ 19
contentBlocks.transliteration:
namaskuruṣva kauntēya tasmai śāntāya vai sadā ।
rudrāya śitikaṇṭhāya kaniṣṭhāya suvarchasē ॥ 19
contentBlocks.translation: Always bow to that peaceful one, O son of Kunti; to Rudra, the blue-throated, the youngest, the brilliantly glowing.

contentBlocks.original:
कपर्दिने करलाय हर्यक्षवरदायच ।
याम्यायरक्तकेशाय सद्वृत्ते शङ्करायच ॥ 20
contentBlocks.transliteration:
kapardinē karaḻāya haryakṣavaradāyacha ।
yāmyāyaraktakēśāya sadvṛttē śaṅkarāyacha ॥ 20
contentBlocks.translation: The matted-locked, the fierce, the granter of boons to devotees, and to the beneficent one; the tawny-eyed, to Yamya (lord of Yama's domain), the red-haired, the pure-conduct, to Shankara.

contentBlocks.original:
काम्याय हरिनेत्राय स्थाणुवे पुरुषायच ।
हरिकेशाय मुण्डाय कनिष्ठाय सुवर्चसे ॥ 21
contentBlocks.transliteration:
kāmyāya harinētrāya sthāṇuvē puruṣāyacha ।
harikēśāya muṇḍāya kaniṣṭhāya suvarchasē ॥ 21
contentBlocks.translation: To the desirable one, the green-eyed, to the immovable Purusha; to the green-haired, the shaven-headed, to the youngest, the brilliantly glowing.

contentBlocks.original:
भास्कराय सुतीर्थाय देवदेवाय रंहसे ।
बहुरूपाय प्रियाय प्रियवाससे ॥ 22
contentBlocks.transliteration:
bhāskarāya sutīrthāya dēvadēvāya raṃhasē ।
bahurūpāya priyāya priyavāsasē ॥ 22
contentBlocks.translation: To the radiant one, to the one of sacred fords, to the god of gods, to the swift; to the many-formed, to the beloved, to the lovely-vestured.

contentBlocks.original:
उष्णीषिणे सुवक्त्राय सहस्राक्षाय मीडुषे ।
गिरीशीय सुशान्ताय पतये चीरवाससे ॥ 23
contentBlocks.transliteration:
uṣṇīṣiṇē suvaktrāya sahasrākṣāya mīḍuṣē ।
girīśīya suśāntāya patayē chīravāsasē ॥ 23
contentBlocks.translation: To the turbaned, to the fine-faced, to the thousand-eyed, to the shower of boons; to the mountain-lord, to the supremely peaceful, to the lord dressed in bark-garments.

contentBlocks.original:
हिरण्यबाहवे राजन्नुग्राय पतयेदिशाम् ।
पर्जन्यपतयेचैव भूतानां पतये नमः ॥ 24
contentBlocks.transliteration:
hiraṇyabāhavē rājannugrāya patayēdiśām ।
parjanyapatayēchaiva bhūtānāṃ patayē namaḥ ॥ 24
contentBlocks.translation: To the golden-armed, O king, to the fierce, to the lord of all directions; and to the lord of rain-clouds, salutations to the lord of all beings.

contentBlocks.original:
वृक्षाणां पतयेचैव गवां च पतये तथा ।
वृक्षैरावृत्तकायाय सेनान्ये मध्यमायच ॥ 25
contentBlocks.transliteration:
vṛkṣāṇāṃ patayēchaiva gavāṃ cha patayē tathā ।
vṛkṣairāvṛttakāyāya sēnānyē madhyamāyacha ॥ 25
contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to the lord of trees, to the lord of cattle; to the one whose body is wrapped in trees, to the army commander, to the middling one.

contentBlocks.original:
स्रुवहस्ताय देवाय धन्विने भार्गवाय च ।
बहुरूपाय विश्वस्य पतये मुञ्जवाससे ॥ 26
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sruvahastāya dēvāya dhanvinē bhārgavāya cha ।
bahurūpāya viśvasya patayē muñjavāsasē ॥ 26
contentBlocks.translation: To the ladle-handed divine one, to the bow-bearer, to the Bhargava; to the many-formed, to the lord of the universe, to the Munja-grass clad.

contentBlocks.original:
सहस्रशिरसे चैव सहस्र नयनायच ।
सहस्रबाहवे चैव सहस्र चरणाय च ॥ 27
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sahasraśirasē chaiva sahasra nayanāyacha ।
sahasrabāhavē chaiva sahasra charaṇāya cha ॥ 27
contentBlocks.translation: To the thousand-headed, to the thousand-eyed; to the thousand-armed, to the thousand-footed.

contentBlocks.original:
शरणं गच्छ कौन्तेय वरदं भुवनेश्वरम् ।
उमापतिं विरूपाक्षं दक्षं यज्ञनिबर्हणम् ॥ 28
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śaraṇaṃ gachCha kauntēya varadaṃ bhuvanēśvaram ।
umāpatiṃ virūpākṣaṃ dakṣaṃ yajñanibarhaṇam ॥ 28
contentBlocks.translation: O Arjuna, take refuge in that boon-granting lord of all worlds; in Uma's consort, the odd-eyed (Virupaksha), the capable one who destroyed Daksha's sacrifice.

contentBlocks.original:
प्रजानां पतिमव्यग्रं भूतानां पतिमव्ययम् ।
कपर्दिनं वृषावर्तं वृषनाभं वृषध्वजम् ॥ 29
contentBlocks.transliteration:
prajānāṃ patimavyagraṃ bhūtānāṃ patimavyayam ।
kapardinaṃ vṛṣāvartaṃ vṛṣanābhaṃ vṛṣadhvajam ॥ 29
contentBlocks.translation: The lord of all people, the untroubled, the imperishable lord of all beings; the matted-locked, the bull-moving, bull-navel, bull-bannered.

contentBlocks.original:
वृषदर्पं वृषपतिं वृषशृङ्गं वृषर्षभम् ।
वृषाकं वृषभोदारं वृषभं वृषभेक्षणम् ॥ 30
contentBlocks.transliteration:
vṛṣadarpaṃ vṛṣapatiṃ vṛṣaśṛṅgaṃ vṛṣarṣabham ।
vṛṣākaṃ vṛṣabhōdāraṃ vṛṣabhaṃ vṛṣabhēkṣaṇam ॥ 30
contentBlocks.translation: The proud bull, the bull-lord, the bull-horned, the bull-best; the bull-form, the mighty bull, Vrishabha, bull-eyed.

contentBlocks.original:
वृषायुधं वृषशरं वृषभूतं महेश्वरम् ।
महोदरं महाकायं द्वीपचर्मनिवासिनम् ॥ 31
contentBlocks.transliteration:
vṛṣāyudhaṃ vṛṣaśaraṃ vṛṣabhūtaṃ mahēśvaram ।
mahōdaraṃ mahākāyaṃ dvīpacharmanivāsinam ॥ 31
contentBlocks.translation: The bull-weaponed, the bull-arrowed, the bull-incarnate, Maheshvara; the great-bellied, the great-bodied, wearing the skin of a great sea creature.

contentBlocks.original:
लोकेशं वरदं मुण्डं ब्राह्मण्यं ब्राह्मणप्रियम् ।
त्रिशूलपाणिं वरदं खड्गचर्मधरं शुभम् ॥ 32
contentBlocks.transliteration:
lōkēśaṃ varadaṃ muṇḍaṃ brāhmaṇyaṃ brāhmaṇapriyam ।
triśūlapāṇiṃ varadaṃ khaḍgacharmadharaṃ śubham ॥ 32
contentBlocks.translation: Lord of the world, the boon-giving, the shaven, the Brahmin, dear to Brahmins; holding the trident, granting boons, bearing the sword and shield, the auspicious one.

contentBlocks.original:
पिनाकिनं खड्गधरं लोकानां पतिमीश्वरम् ।
प्रपद्ये शरणं देवं शरण्यं चीरवासनम् ॥ 33
contentBlocks.transliteration:
pinākinaṃ khaḍgadharaṃ lōkānāṃ patimīśvaram ।
prapadyē śaraṇaṃ dēvaṃ śaraṇyaṃ chīravāsanam ॥ 33
contentBlocks.translation: Pinakini (bearer of the Pinaka bow), sword-bearer, lord of all people; I take refuge in that lord, the divine protector, the dressed in bark garments.

contentBlocks.original:
नमस्तस्मै सुरेशाय यस्य वैश्रवणस्सखा ।
सुवाससे नमो नित्यं सुव्रताय सुधन्विने ॥ 34
contentBlocks.transliteration:
namastasmai surēśāya yasya vaiśravaṇassakhā ।
suvāsasē namō nityaṃ suvratāya sudhanvinē ॥ 34
contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to that lord of gods whose friend is Kubera; salutations always to the finely clad, to the one of pure vows, to the excellent bow-bearer.

contentBlocks.original:
धनुर्धराय देवाय प्रियधन्वाय धन्विने ।
धन्वन्तराय धनुषे धन्वाचार्याय ते नमः ॥ 35
contentBlocks.transliteration:
dhanurdharāya dēvāya priyadhanvāya dhanvinē ।
dhanvantarāya dhanuṣē dhanvāchāryāya tē namaḥ ॥ 35
contentBlocks.translation: To the bow-bearing divine one, to the beloved bow-bearer, the archer; to the one like Dhanvantari, to the bow itself, to the great teacher of archery — salutations.

contentBlocks.original:
उग्रायुधाय देवाय नमस्सुरवराय च ।
नमोऽस्तु बहुरूपाय नमस्ते बहुदन्विने ॥ 36
contentBlocks.transliteration:
ugrāyudhāya dēvāya namassuravarāya cha ।
namō'stu bahurūpāya namastē bahudanvinē ॥ 36
contentBlocks.translation: To the fierce-weaponed divine one, salutations to the best of gods; salutations to the many-formed, salutations to you the great bow-bearer.

contentBlocks.original:
नमोऽस्तु स्थाणवे नित्यन्नमस्तस्मै सुधन्विने ।
नमोऽस्तु त्रिपुरघ्नाय भवघ्नाय च वै नमः ॥ 37
contentBlocks.transliteration:
namō'stu sthāṇavē nityannamastasmai sudhanvinē ।
namō'stu tripuraghnāya bhavaghnāya cha vai namaḥ ॥ 37
contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to the immovable pillar, salutations to the excellent bow-bearer; salutations to the destroyer of the triple city, and salutations to the destroyer of samsara.

contentBlocks.original:
वनस्पतीनां पतये नराणां पतये नमः ।
मातॄणां पतये चैव गणानां पतये नमः ॥ 38
contentBlocks.transliteration:
vanaspatīnāṃ patayē narāṇāṃ patayē namaḥ ।
mātṝṇāṃ patayē chaiva gaṇānāṃ patayē namaḥ ॥ 38
contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to the lord of trees, salutations to the lord of men; salutations to the lord of the Matrika goddesses, salutations to the lord of ganas.

contentBlocks.original:
गवां च पतये नित्यं यज्ञानां पतये नमः ।
अपां च पतये नित्यं देवानां पतये नमः ॥ 39
contentBlocks.transliteration:
gavāṃ cha patayē nityaṃ yajñānāṃ patayē namaḥ ।
apāṃ cha patayē nityaṃ dēvānāṃ patayē namaḥ ॥ 39
contentBlocks.translation: Salutations always to the lord of cattle, salutations to the lord of sacrifices; salutations always to the lord of waters, salutations to the lord of gods.

contentBlocks.original:
पूष्णो दन्तविनाशाय त्र्यक्षाय वरदायच ।
हराय नीलकण्ठाय स्वर्णकेशाय वै नमः ॥ 40
contentBlocks.transliteration:
pūṣṇō dantavināśāya tryakṣāya varadāyacha ।
harāya nīlakaṇṭhāya svarṇakēśāya vai namaḥ ॥ 40
contentBlocks.translation: Destroyer of Pushan's teeth, the three-eyed, the granter of boons; to Hara, the blue-throated, to the golden-haired — salutations.
